title: Implement wizard core detection and installation plan
outOfScope: Interactive wizard I/O, localization, or routing
Calibration Wizard behavior or session state transitions
Changing the existing transactional write, branch, rollback, or PR lifecycle
Adding third-party runtime dependencies or publishing a release
Rewriting historical archived Work Item evidence

sources.reason: Defines the Work Item 3 detection and Installation Plan requirements.
Defines repository governance and architecture boundaries.
Evidence that the predecessor PR was merged and closed before this branch started.
Authoritative installer transaction, adoption, upgrade, and conflict behavior to preserve.
Existing installation fact bundle and ownership vocabulary.
Existing Git subprocess adapter and argument-list safety boundary.

riskAssessment.reason: Read-only detection is a new adapter boundary around installation behavior; incorrect facts could mislead a future wizard, while transaction behavior must remain unchanged.

executionDecision.reason: Predecessor lightweight-verification-soft-gates is merged, archived, closed, and origin/main is synchronized.

rawUserRequest: 用户已授权按计划串行执行全部 Work Item。继续执行 wizard-core-detection-plan：完成只读安装事实检测与 Installation Plan、测试、完整治理验证、独立 PR、合并、归档、ai-close-work-item、分支清理和默认分支同步后再进入下一个 Work Item。

intent.problem: The future installation wizard needs trustworthy, reusable facts about a target repository and a deterministic Installation Plan before any write or transaction begins; those facts are currently scattered across installer code and are not exposed through a typed read-only boundary.
constraints: Detection must not mutate the target repository or duplicate transaction, branch, rollback, or Work Item state transitions.
Use standard-library Python and subprocess argument lists; preserve existing deterministic installer behavior.
New Adoption and Upgrade remain distinct readiness modes and dangerous actions remain human-confirmed.
intent.rationale: A typed read-only adapter gives the future wizard one deterministic source of facts while keeping the existing Installer authoritative for writes.

declaredIntent.summary: Expose trustworthy read-only repository and toolchain facts and a deterministic Installation Plan for the future human-controlled wizard.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: New Adoption can inspect a repository without requiring an existing active Work Item
scenarioCoverage.reason: Detection facts and readiness model will explicitly distinguish bootstrap from upgrade.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Upgrade detects active Work Items, dirty state, tracked hygiene files, and conflicts without writing
scenarioCoverage.reason: Tests will use injected Git reads and assert no target mutation.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Missing tools, runtime signals, stacks, symlink risk, remote/default branch, and deterministic plan serialization are represented
scenarioCoverage.reason: Typed facts and stable JSON serialization cover each plan field.

acceptance: Typed read-only detection exposes Git, remote/default branch, worktree cleanliness, tracked hygiene, symlink risk, Python/Git/Make/curl/POSIX tools, runtime presence, stack signals, and New Adoption versus Upgrade readiness.
Installation Plan contains facts, recommendation, impact, examples, write boundary, expected result, stop condition, and checklist, and serializes deterministically.
Detection uses safe argument-list subprocess calls and does not mutate the target repository; existing transactional installer behavior remains authoritative.
Unit tests prove New Adoption does not require an existing Work Item and Upgrade detects active Work Items/conflicts, including unknown or unavailable tools.
AI Change Summary records implementation, verification, guidelines, checkpoints, known gaps, and residual risk without overclaiming.
guidelines: Keep detection read-only and preserve the existing Installer transaction boundary.
Use standard-library Python and subprocess argument lists; never shell=True with user-controlled input.
Document public detection and Installation Plan APIs.
Do not rewrite historical Work Item evidence or introduce unrelated wizard behavior.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record evidence before implementation and archival.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized serial execution of this governed Work Item.

rollbackNote: Revert only this Work Item's diff; preserve predecessor archive evidence and installer transaction behavior.

budgetImpact.reason: Measured start baseline with bounded implementation and no historical evidence edits.
